subject: SCIENCE
title: Animal Parts at Work
objective: Explain that animal body parts can support different functions.
introduction: The same kind of part may have different functions across species. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Animal Parts at Work”.
A key word used in the lesson “Animal Parts at Work”.
A key word used in the lesson “Animal Parts at Work”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.

examples: tails may aid balance or communication

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

fits: tails may aid balance or communication
doesNotFit: All beaks eat the same food

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Which part often helps a fish swim?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. fur and antlers do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

pairs.left: tails may aid balance or communication

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Animal Parts at Work”?

options.text: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.

items.explanation: The correct response is “The same kind of part may have different functions across species.” because it follows the lesson explanation: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.
The correct response is “fins can help swimming” because it follows the lesson explanation: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Every tail has one function” because it follows the lesson explanation: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.

items.prompt: Which part often helps a fish swim?
items.explanation: The correct response is “fins” because it follows the lesson explanation: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: tails may aid balance or communication
All beaks eat the same food

items.explanation: The correct response is “tails may aid balance or communication” because it follows the lesson explanation: The same kind of part may have different functions across species.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Animal Parts at Work; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Animal Parts at Work.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
